commit.message:
fix(charges): Do not touch charges when updating charge children in b… (#5324)

Summary
- Add Charge.no_touching to Charges::UpdateChildrenService to prevent
cascading touch: true writes from ChargeFilter → Charge during
batch cascade updates
Context
ChargeFilter has belongs_to :charge, touch: true, meaning every filter
save/touch triggers an UPDATE on the parent charge row. During a
batch cascade update of 20 children, each with N filters, this causes
~2N redundant UPDATEs per child charge (one from filter.save!, one
from the explicit filter.touch), plus PaperTrail version inserts for
each touched charge.
This write amplification inside a single transaction causes long-held
row locks. When two UpdateChildrenJob runs overlap (e.g., rapid
successive edits to the parent charge), the second batch waits for the
first's transaction lock — we observed individual UPDATE
charge_filters queries waiting over 1h30.
Plan.no_touching was already in place for the same reason. This adds
Charge.no_touching following the same pattern. Normal charge.save!
still works (it's not a touch), so all attribute changes are persisted —
only the redundant cascading touches are skipped.
Test plan

- Existing Charges::UpdateChildrenService specs pass (6 examples, 0
failures)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Use active station profile in storage workbench smoke test (#96)

The smoke test was always building sample QSOs with hardcoded
SampleProtoFactory constants (K7DBG, CN87up, etc.) instead of
using the engine's active station profile.

Add an optional StationProfile parameter to RunSmokeTestAsync.
When present, it overwrites StationCallsign and StationSnapshot
on the sample QSO with real profile data. The Razor page passes
the active profile from DebugWorkbenchState. SampleProtoFactory
remains a pure sample generator, unaffected by this change.

commit.message:
feat: add cache-aware cost estimation for Claude and OpenCode sessions (#607)

Track cache token breakdowns (cachedReadTokens, cacheCreationTokens) in
ModelUsage so calculateEstimatedCost() can apply Anthropic prompt-caching
discount rates instead of full input prices.

Key changes:
- types.ts: add optional cachedReadTokens and cacheCreationTokens fields
  to ModelUsage; add optional cachedInputCostPerMillion and
  cacheCreationCostPerMillion fields to ModelPricing
- modelPricing.json: add cache pricing for all Claude models
  (cache read = 10% of input, cache creation = 125% of input) and
  gpt-4o / gpt-4o-mini (cache read = 50% of input)
- tokenEstimation.ts: update calculateEstimatedCost to apply per-type
  rates; inputTokens stays as full total so token count displays are
  unaffected
- claudedesktop.ts, claudecode.ts: populate cachedReadTokens and
  cacheCreationTokens from cache_read_input_tokens /
  cache_creation_input_tokens in Anthropic API usage fields
- opencode.ts: populate cache fields from tokens.cache.read /
  tokens.cache.write in OpenCode DB message data
- extension.ts: bump CACHE_VERSION to 39
- README.md: document new cache pricing fields and source survey table

Fixes #604

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Authenticode-sign Windows updater bundle

The previous workflow signed the NSIS installer exe after Tauri had
already packaged the unsigned exe into the .nsis.zip updater bundle.
Users updating via the in-app updater would get an unsigned installer,
triggering SmartScreen warnings.

Now the flow is:
1. Authenticode-sign the NSIS installer exe
2. Re-create the .nsis.zip from the signed exe
3. Re-sign the .nsis.zip with Tauri's Ed25519 key

commit.message:
Port @azure/postgresql-auth package from postgres-entra-auth repo (#37647)

## Description

Migrates the standalone
[azure-postgresql-auth](https://github.com/Azure/postgres-entra-auth/tree/main/javascript)
library into the monorepo as `@azure/postgresql-auth` - a new Azure SDK
client package providing Entra ID authentication for PostgreSQL
connections via both `pg` (node-postgres) and Sequelize clients.

### Key changes:
- **Source:** TypeScript implementation with full type safety
- `getEntraTokenPassword()` - acquires Entra ID token for use as
PostgreSQL password
- `configureEntraIdAuth()` - registers Sequelize `beforeConnect` hook
for automatic token-based auth
- **Build:** Uses `warp` build system with 4 targets (browser,
react-native, esm, commonjs)
- **Tests:** 19 unit tests with vitest (all passing)
- **Peer deps:** `pg` (>=8.0.0) and `sequelize` (>=6.0.0) as optional
peer dependencies
- **CI:** New `sdk/postgresql/ci.yml` data-plane pipeline
- **CODEOWNERS:** Updated

### References
- Standalone source:
https://github.com/Azure/postgres-entra-auth/tree/main/javascript
- .NET reference: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/55231
## Release Plan Details
- Release Plan:
https://web.powerapps.com/apps/821ab569-ae60-420d-8264-d7b5d5ef734c?release-plan-id=0213048b-d1f0-f011-8407-000d3a3b8d97

https://dev.azure.com/azure-sdk/fe81d705-3c06-41e5-bf7c-5ebea18efe89/_workitems/edit/30201

commit.message:
feat: add generated-by watermark to all templates; use content-based uninstall detection

Add \generated-by: .principles\ to the frontmatter of all four tool
templates (claude, copilot-cli, copilot-ide, codex). Every file
installed by install.sh now carries this watermark.

Update uninstall.sh to scan installed directories and remove files by
watermark instead of matching current command names. This makes
uninstall version-agnostic: files from renamed commands are cleaned up
correctly without needing to know their old names. Legacy command names
(LEGACY_COMMAND_NAMES) are still checked as a fallback for pre-watermark
installs.

Document the watermark in DESIGN.md (Consistent Frontmatter table and
Uninstall section) and record the change in CHANGELOG.md under
[Unreleased].

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

4.6) on behalf of @johnallen3d.

## Summary

Implements the two formatting changes described in #32.

**ON/USING inline** — join keyword, table ref, alias, and condition are
all on one line. No more `\n ON` break.

**Alias alignment** — when all `FROM`/`JOIN` entries in a block are
simple table refs, aliases are padded so they start at the same column.
The column is `max(keyword_width + table_name_width) + 1`. If any entry
uses a subquery, alignment is skipped for the whole block but ON stays
inline.

**`AS` omitted from table aliases** — the `AS` keyword is dropped
between table name and alias on `FROM`/`JOIN` lines. Column aliases in
`SELECT` lists are unchanged.

## Changes

- `src/jarify/generator.py` — new helpers (`_join_keyword`,
`_table_alias_str`, `_inline_on_sql`, `_table_ref_only_sql`,
`_compute_join_align_width`) and overrides for `join_sql`, `from_sql`;
`select_sql` now sets `_join_alias_col` before delegating to super
- `tests/fixtures/joins/aligned_joins.{input,expected}.sql` — new
fixture covering a multi-table aligned block
- Updated `bare_join`, `multi_join`, `real_world`, and `basic_cte`
expected fixtures for inline ON and dropped `AS`
- `tests/test_formatter.py` — new `TestJoinFormatting` class (6 unit
tests)
- `docs/sql-style-guide.md` — updated "Table aliases" rule; added "JOIN
formatting" section

Resolves #32

commit.message:
feat(iac): add snapshosts for microos-based vms for k3s cluster (#45)

* feat(iac): add snapshosts for microos-based vms for k3s cluster

I decided to migrate from hard and costly talos based cluster withotu
proper autoscaling features in the terraform modules, to the lightweight
version of kubernetes from SUSE, called k3s.

That switch allows me to keep the kubernetes benefits without the
caveats of maintaing huge cluster. K3S uses much less resoruces than k8s
control-plane, so it's better to current side-project vibe of my
infrastructure.

In later stages it will restore the previous setup in most way.
Hopefully it will be much cheaper.

* fix(iac): apply sugestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ci): do not run build snapshot on the pr level

We shouldn't run the snapshots builder on the PR level, as we want to
avoid building the snapshot upon PR creation.

* fix(iac): resolve incorrect url creation when calling hcloud

* fix(iac): use underscores instead of hyphens in output key

* fix(iac): Add timeout for 10 minutes upon creation

* fix(iac): use accept-new for host key checking

* fix(iac): add SSH key management and fix authentication issues

- Generate and upload temporary SSH key for server authentication
- Remove rescue mode in favor of SSH key-based authentication
- Add comprehensive cleanup of servers, SSH keys, and temp files
- Fix API path issues (remove leading slashes)
- Add jq dependency validation
- Fix SELinux path typo (/ver/lib -> /var/lib)
- Use StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new for better security

This fixes the script hanging on SSH connections and ensures proper
resource cleanup on both success and failure scenarios.

* fix(iac): use rescue mode and create NetworkManager directory

- Re-enable rescue mode which has qemu-img pre-installed
- Create /etc/NetworkManager directory before touching config file
- Update step numbering to reflect actual process (1/9 through 9/9)

* fix(iac): install qemu-utils in rescue environment before writing image

Hetzner's rescue mode doesn't have qemu-img pre-installed, so we need
to install qemu-utils package before attempting to convert and write
the MicroOS image to disk. Also use sync before reboot for safety.

* fix(iac): use sysrq trigger for immediate reboot after disk write

After writing MicroOS image to /dev/sda, the running system's binaries
are corrupted and cannot be executed. Use kernel sysrq trigger for
immediate reboot instead of sync/sleep/reboot which will fail.

* fix(iac): use background reboot with nohup after disk write

Use nohup with sleep and reboot -f in background to ensure the SSH
connection closes cleanly before reboot. Increase wait time to 10s
to give the system more time to reboot into MicroOS.

* fix(iac): enable sysrq and trigger kernel reboot after image write

Run qemu-img in background with kernel sysrq reboot trigger. Enable
sysrq first, then trigger immediate kernel reboot after image write.
Increase wait time to 30s for image write and reboot to complete.

* fix(iac): remove background process and use direct sysrq reboot

Remove background subshell that was causing the script to hang.
Execute sysrq reboot directly after image write completes.
Change set -ex to set -e to avoid verbose output during reboot.

* fix(iac): separate image writing from reboot to avoid corruption

Split the image write and reboot into two separate SSH commands:
1. First command writes the image completely (with normal run_remote)
2. Second command triggers reboot only (with run_remote_disconnect_ok)

This prevents the reboot from interrupting the image write process
and ensures binaries aren't corrupted before the reboot trigger.

* fix(iac): inject SSH key into MicroOS image before reboot

After writing the MicroOS image to disk, mount the root partition
and inject the SSH authorized_keys from the rescue system. This
ensures SSH access works after rebooting into MicroOS.

Mount /dev/sda3 (MicroOS root partition), create .ssh directory,
copy authorized_keys, and set proper permissions before unmounting.

* fix(iac): write image to temp file, inject SSH key, then write to disk

Convert qcow2 to raw format in /tmp first, mount it via loop device,
inject SSH key, then write the modified image to /dev/sda using dd.

This prevents filesystem corruption by not mounting /dev/sda after
it has been overwritten by the image.

* fix(iac): improve loop device partition detection and add debugging

- Use losetup --show -P to properly create partition devices
- Add partprobe to ensure kernel recognizes partitions
- Add debug output to list partitions
- Try both p3 and p2 for root partition
- Add error handling if no partition found

* fix(ci): failing script for creation of snapshots

The script was not correctly booting into the rescue mode, now it's
fixed and working fine

commit.message:
Fix crossed book: snapshot catch-up MIN, diagnostic stats

Root cause: TrackSnapshotHeader used MAX(LastMsgSeqNumProcessed)
across instruments instead of MIN. Instruments captured early in the
snapshot cycle lost incrementals between MIN and MAX, causing phantom
orders and persistently crossed books.

Fix: use MIN so all instruments receive all incrementals after the
earliest snapshot capture point. Redundant replays for later-captured
instruments are handled gracefully by existing order logic.

Additions:
- /stats endpoint: order counters, gap stats, crossed book list
- BookManager diagnostic counters (adds/updates/deletes/deleteNotFound)
- ChannelHandler counters (processed/duplicates/gaps)
- Crossed-book transition detection with logging (first 20)
- ChannelHandlerProvider wired in Program.cs
